steps.title: Identify what the blank requires
steps.content: The sentence discusses two distinct samples (sample 15 and sample 18), each with its own set of odors and its own primary descriptors. The blank needs a phrase showing that both "samples" and "odors" are plural possessives — plurals that own something.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘sample's odors’: Uses the singular possessive "sample's," indicating only one sample. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘samples' odor's’: Correctly marks "samples" as plural possessive but incorrectly marks "odor" as singular possessive. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘samples odors'’: Leaves "samples" without a possessive marker entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘samples' odors'’: Both words are plural nouns ending in s with only an apostrophe added — the correct form for plural possessives in both cases. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
